journal_entry.description: Board declared cash dividend payable in 30 days
journal_entry.rationale: When a dividend is declared, the corporation incurs a legal obligation to pay. Retained Earnings (equity) is reduced by debiting Dividends Declared, and a liability (Dividends Payable) is created. The dividend will be paid in 30 days, at which time the liability will be settled.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Dividend declaration creates a liability and reduces equity. Debit Dividends Declared (dividend increase, slot b), credit Dividends Payable (liability increase, slot a). No tax applies to dividend declarations.
